zh.Wilden, Bedfordshire, England, United Ki: 英國英格蘭貝德福爾維爾登
zh.Friars Minor, Bedford, Bedfordshire: 英國貝德福爾貝德福德
zh.Corby Glen, Lincolnshire, England, Unite: 英國英格蘭林可爾史爾可比 格倫
zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Peache於大約1251年出生在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾可比 格倫時，她的父親Gilbert de Pecche32歲，母親Maud Hastings31歲。她於1272年2月10日與Nicholas de Criol II在英國結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1319年3月31日在英國英格蘭貝德福爾享年69歲，埋葬在英國貝德福爾貝德福德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Peache于大约1251年出生在英国英格兰林可爾史爾可比 格倫时，她的父亲Gilbert de Pecche32岁，母亲Maud Hastings31岁。她于1272年2月10日与Nicholas de Criol II在英国结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1319年3月31日在英国英格兰貝德福爾享年69岁，埋葬在英国貝德福爾貝德福德。

nameMeanings.name: Peache
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Peach
meanings: English: nickname from Old French pecch(i)et, pech(i)é (from Latin peccatum) ‘fault, sin’. Compare Petch and Peachey .
Probably an Americanized form of German   Pietsch  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Peache was born about 1251, in Corby Glen, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Gilbert de Pecche, was 32 and her mother, Maud Hastings, was 31. She married Nicholas de Criol II on 10 February 1272, in England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 31 March 1319, in Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 69, and was buried in Bedford, Bedfordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Peache est née environ 1251, à Corby Glen, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Gilbert de Pecche, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Maud Hastings, avait 31 ans. Elle a épousé Nicholas de Criol II le 10 février 1272, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 31 mars 1319, à Bedfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 69 ans, et a été enterrée à Bedford, Bedfordshire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Peacheが約1251年にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアコルビー グレンで生まれた時，父Gilbert de Peccheは32歳，母Maud Hastingsは31歳であった。1272年2月10日にイギリスでNicholas de Criol IIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1319年3月31日にイギリスイギリスベドフォードシュアイアで69歳で死去し，イギリスベドフォードシュアイアベドフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Peache(이)가 대략 1251년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 코르비 글렌에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Gilbert de Pecche(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud Hastings(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 1272년 2월 10일 영국에서 Nicholas de Criol II(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1319년 3월 31일 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르에서 69세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 베드퍼드실르 베드퍼드에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Nicholas de Criol II em 10 de fevereiro de 1272, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Peache родилась около 1251 г. в местности корби глен, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Gilbert de Pecche было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Maud Hastings, 31. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Nicholas de Criol II 10 февраля 1272 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 31 марта 1319 г. в местности бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 69 лет и была похоронена в местности бедфорд, бедфордшир, Англия.
